project.title: RIC
project.one_liner: Legacy PHP web application for accounting and records management.
project.description: Server-rendered PHP application with modules for movements, people, accounts, companies, users, and reports, backed by MySQL-driven pages and AJAX endpoints.

prerequisites: PHP runtime with legacy mysql_* compatibility
Web server that serves PHP entry points
setup_steps: This repository does not include a one-command local setup; review Connections/ric.php, Connections/database.php, login.php, and index.php before attempting to run it.

Several tracked modules reference missing connection files (Connections/sigma.php, Connections/horizonte.php, Connections/productores.php), so a complete local run cannot be verified from this checkout.

cv_bullets.bullet: Maintained a server-rendered PHP application with authenticated login, session handling, and access-restricted pages.
cv_bullets.notes: Login form, session start, and access checks are explicit in the tracked code.

cv_bullets.bullet: Worked on a multi-module back-office UI covering movements, people, accounts, companies, users, and reports.
cv_bullets.notes: The shared navigation and top-level entry points expose the application surface directly.

cv_bullets.bullet: Implemented server-side DataTables workflows for paging, filtering, and searching records from PHP JSON endpoints.
cv_bullets.notes: UI pages initialize DataTables in server-side mode and point them at PHP endpoints that return JSON.

cv_bullets.bullet: Implemented accounting policy and payment persistence flows backed by MySQL tables such as polizas and pagos.
cv_bullets.notes: Insert and update queries for policies and payments are explicit in the server-side handlers.

cv_bullets.bullet: Added role-aware and company-aware filtering to movement and report endpoints based on the logged-in user and assigned companies.
cv_bullets.notes: Endpoint conditions branch on user access level, selected company, and company assignments.

cv_bullets.bullet: Implemented document and media upload handlers for people, companies, policy PDFs/XML, and user images using PHP file uploads.
cv_bullets.notes: The tracked handlers use move_uploaded_file and persist uploaded asset metadata in MySQL tables.

cv_bullets.bullet: Automated company onboarding by creating the company record and seeding related account structures.
cv_bullets.notes: The company creation handler inserts into empresa and then creates multiple cuentas records.

cv_bullets.bullet: Built user-to-company assignment flows with primary-company logic and supporting admin grids.
cv_bullets.notes: The UI and handlers explicitly support assigning, listing, and prioritizing companies per user.

cv_bullets.bullet: Implemented person-to-account linking and document management inside the people module.
cv_bullets.notes: The people screen includes account assignment workflows and a dedicated documents grid.

cv_bullets.bullet: Added export-oriented reporting views with copy, CSV, Excel, PDF, print, and column-visibility actions.
cv_bullets.notes: The report page loads DataTables Buttons and wires export actions to summary endpoints.

red_flags.issue: Tracked connection files contain hard-coded database credentials.
red_flags.notes: Credentials were intentionally redacted from the generated documentation and should not be published.

red_flags.issue: The checkout appears incomplete for a reproducible local run because several referenced connection files are missing.
red_flags.notes: Tracked code references Connections/horizonte.php, Connections/sigma.php, and Connections/productores.php, but only ric.php and database.php exist under Connections/.

red_flags.issue: The codebase depends heavily on deprecated mysql_* APIs.
red_flags.notes: Running on modern PHP versions may require code changes or legacy compatibility support.
